title: Guzmania candelabrum
taxonomy: Guzmania candelabrum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Bromeliaceae. This family of plants is commonly known as the pineapple family, and it consists of over 57 genera and more than 3,000 species. Guzmania candelabrum is a type of epiphyte, meaning it grows on other plants or objects for support, and is native to Central and South America. As a member of the Bromeliaceae family, Guzmania candelabrum is closely related to other tropical plants such as pineapples, air plants, and orchids. Its classification within the family is further specified by its genus, Guzmania, which is a group of tropical plants known for their colorful inflorescences and epiphytic growth habits.

intro: Guzmania candelabrum, also known as the Candelabrum Guzmania, is a type of epiphytic bromeliad plant native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and pneumonia. The bioactive constituents of Guzmania candelabrum include guazulene, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
